The 2025 FIM JuniorGP World Championship is the fourteenth season of the class and the fourth season after leaving the historical connection to CEV. The season will be held over 12 races at 7 meetings, beginning on 4 May at Estoril and ending on 23 November at Valencia. The provisional calendar was announced in October 2024. The championship's scoring system awards points to the top fifteen finishers, and riders must finish the race to earn points. The riders' championship is the main focus of the championship.

The 2025 UEC European Track Championships – Men's sprint will be held on 14 and 15 February 2025. The qualification for the championship will be held on 14 February, and the final will be held on 15 February. The top 4 riders will qualify for the 1/8 finals, and the rest of the riders will qualify for the 1/16 finals. The heat winners will advance to the quarterfinals, and the winners of the quarterfinals will advance to the semifinals. The winners of the semifinals will advance to the finals.

The 2025 Spanish Motocross Championship season is the 67th Spanish Motocross Championship season. The series has seven rounds across the country, running from February to October. The Elite-MX1 class has José Butrón as the reigning champion, while the Elite-MX2 class has no defending champion as Gerard Congost moved up to the Elite-MX1 class for the 2025 season. Due to adverse track conditions, the Saturday programme at the opening round in Talavera de la Reina was cancelled, meaning no points for qualifying were awarded.

The FIS Freestyle Ski and Snowboarding World Championships 2025 – Men's parallel slalom was held on 22 March 2025. The qualification for the championship was held at 09:40, and the elimination round was held after the first run.

The FIS Nordic World Ski Championships 2025 – Women's individual normal hill will be held on 27 and 28 February 202
